Title: Marin Fairfax 2
Description:
Mid-Tier Fitness Hybrid · 700C

The middle-child Fairfax is a big step up in capability. You get a Series 2 aluminum frame with internal cable routing, an aluminum straight-blade fork, a Shimano 2x8-speed drivetrain with sub-compact 46/30T gearing (true low gears for real hills), and — crucially at this price — Tektro M275 hydraulic disc brakes. Wide-volume 35mm puncture-protected Vee Zilent tires add comfort and all-weather capability. Hits the magic £1,000 / $1,000 price point and punches above its weight.

Title: Marin Gestalt X10
Description:
Aggressive Drop-Bar Gravel / Drop-Bar MTB · 700C/650B

The Gestalt X takes Marin's Beyond Road Geometry to the next level of aggressive dirt exploration. Mountain-bike-inspired numbers — a 67.5° head angle, long wheelbase, short 50mm stem, and wide 480mm flared bar — mean it carves technical terrain like nothing else in the drop-bar world. Room for massive tires: up to 700x50mm or 650Bx54mm. Uses a wide-range 1x10 microSHIFT Advent drivetrain for simplicity, has dropper-post routing, and dual-piston Tektro mechanical discs. A true drop-bar mountain bike.

Title: Transition Sentinel
Description:
Trail Bike (Carbon or Alloy) · 29" or Mixed

Transition's best-selling trail bike, and arguably the sweet-spot 29er on the market. 150mm rear travel, 160mm fork, designed to climb all day in service of the descent. The V3 (2025+) brings mixed-wheel compatibility (flip-chip), Transition's internal "BOOM Box" storage (on carbon frames), size-adjusted chainstays, updated cable routing, and UDH. Available as alloy or carbon; carbon gets internal storage. Horst-link suspension means excellent traction and mid-stroke support. Offered in Eagle 70 and Eagle 90 (T-Type Transmission) builds.

At a Glance

  • Frame: Carbon Fiber or 6061 Aluminum, 150mm rear travel, 29" or Mixed (27.5" rear) via flip chip, Horst-Link suspension, BSA Threaded BB, SRAM UDH (carbon), BOOM Box internal storage (carbon only)
  • Fork: RockShox Lyrik or Fox 36, 160mm travel (180mm max compatible)
  • Rear Shock: RockShox Super Deluxe or Fox Float X, 205x60mm trunnion (upgradable to 65mm stroke for 160mm travel)
  • Drivetrain: SRAM Eagle 90 T-Type Transmission (or Eagle 70), 12-speed
  • Brakes: SRAM Motive/DB8 or equivalent, 4-piston

Title: Pivot Shuttle AM Ride Eagle 70/90
Description:
Full-Power Carbon E-MTB (All-Mountain) · 29"

Pivot's Gen 2 Shuttle AM for 2025 is one of the lightest, most natural-riding full-power e-MTBs you can buy. Ultralight Hollow Core carbon frame, 150mm of dw-link rear travel, and a 160mm Fox 36 Performance fork up front. The Bosch Performance Line CX (Gen 5) motor cranks out 100Nm of torque / 750W peak power, fed by a long-range 800Wh integrated battery. The Ride Eagle 70/90 build comes with a mechanical SRAM Eagle 90 Transmission — simple, reliable, and crisp — with DB8 4-piston brakes, 200mm rotors, DT Swiss Hybrid H1900 wheels, and Maxxis EXO+ tires. One of the most balanced e-MTBs on the market.

At a Glance

  • Frame: Ultralight Hollow Core Carbon, 150mm rear travel, 29" wheels (Mullet compatible via high flip chip), dw-link suspension, Pivot Cable Port System, SRAM UDH, BSA threaded BB
  • Motor: Bosch Performance Line CX (Gen 5), 100Nm torque, 750W peak power
  • Battery: Bosch PowerTube 800Wh (integrated)
  • Fork: Fox 36 Performance, 160mm travel, 44mm offset
  • Rear Shock: Fox Float X Performance, trunnion mount

Title: Transition Regulator CX Eagle 90
Description:
Full-Power Carbon E-MTB (All-Mountain) · Mixed (29"/27.5")

Transition's take on a motorized Sentinel — an all-mountain workhorse with an intelligent mixed-wheel platform. 150mm rear / 160mm front travel, carbon chassis, and a Bosch Performance Line CX Gen 5 motor putting out 85 Nm of torque (100Nm with the software upgrade). The 600Wh in-frame battery is supplemented by an optional 250Wh PowerMore range extender, pushing total capacity to 850Wh. Flip-chip geometry adjustment and SRAM Eagle 90 Transmission / Transmission-level parts throughout. A party in the woods, with power.

Title: Surly Sorceress
Description:
Steel Modern-Trail Hardtail · 29" (27.5" on XS) · Elderberry Tonic (deep purple)

Surly's brand-new (2026) aggressive trail hardtail, and their first return to the hardtail market with genuinely progressive geometry. Modern and rowdy: 65° head angle, 76° seat angle, built around a 140mm fork, with long front-center for stability at speed, and a patent-pending Surly DUH dropout system that lets you run SRAM UDH Transmission drivetrains OR single-speed (via swappable chips). Low standover and maximized dropper-post insertion accommodate riders from 4'9" to 6'6". Frame is 100% Natch chromoly steel — size-tuned — with double-butted main triangle and trumpet-flared tubes. The Eagle 90 is the top build, available in the striking Elderberry Tonic (deep purple) colorway.

At a Glance

  • Frame: 100% Surly Chromoly (Natch Tubing), double-butted main triangle, trumpet-flared tubes, TIG welded, E.D. coated, silicone chainstay protector, patent-pending DUH dropout system
  • Fork: 140mm travel, 15x110 Boost, ~44mm offset (designed around 140mm; plays with 130-150mm)
  • Drivetrain: SRAM Eagle 90 T-Type Transmission, 12-speed (mechanical)
  • Brakes: SRAM DB6 / DB8 Hydraulic, 4-piston
  • Tires: Teravail or similar 29x2.6" / 27.5x2.6" (XS)

Title: Surly Bridge Club
Description:
Steel All-Road / Off-Road Tourer · 27.5" · Lingering (deep red)

Surly's entry-level do-it-all tourer, and a bikepacking/commuting legend. The Bridge Club is for those multi-surface outings — planned or unplanned — that mix dirt and pavement in unexpected proportions. 100% 4130 chromoly steel frame (double-butted Natch main triangle, TIG-welded, ED-coated) with Surly's matching Cocktail chromoly fork. Rolls on high-volume 27.5" x 2.4" tires (frame clears up to 27.5x2.8" or 700x47). The November 2023 update brought a modern Shimano CUES 11-speed drivetrain and Tektro hydraulic discs. Mounts everywhere — racks, fenders, Anything Cages, the works. "Lingering" is Surly's deep wine-red colorway.

At a Glance

  • Frame: 100% 4130 Chromoly, Natch tubing, double-butted main triangle, TIG welded, ED coated
  • Fork: Surly Cocktail Fork, 4130 Chromoly double-butted tapered blades, TIG welded, ED coated, three-pack Anything Cage mounts on each blade
  • Drivetrain: Shimano CUES 1x11 (32T ring, 11-50T cassette)
  • Brakes: Tektro Hydraulic Disc, 160/160mm
  • Rims: WTB i29 or similar, tubeless compatible

Title: Transition Spire Carbon Eagle 90
Description:
Carbon Enduro / Big Mountain · 29"

The Spire is Transition's "nimble bruiser" — a 170mm-travel carbon enduro weapon that blends DH-bike capability with mid-travel efficiency (Marco Osborne won the Stone King Rally on one). The Eagle 90 build wraps the carbon frame (Japanese Toray fiber, EPS molding process) in a SRAM Eagle 90 Transmission drivetrain, with Fox Performance Elite 38 fork and Float X shock, and the geometry-adjusting flip chip that makes this a uniquely versatile big bike. Climbs better than you'd expect, descends like a freight train. Get inSpired.

  • Drivetrain: SRAM Eagle 90 T-Type Transmission, 12-speed (mechanical)
  • Brakes: SRAM DB8 (or Motive), 4-piston, 200mm rotors (F/R)
